1900s.title: Long-run UK RPI coverage
1900s.body: The UK prototype uses the ONS long-run RPI series, which gives broad historical coverage for pound-denominated purchasing power estimates.

1940s.title: UK wartime controls
1940s.body: Wartime rationing, controls, and shortages shaped British household costs. A price index captures official prices, not every hardship of scarcity.

1970s.title: UK inflation volatility
1970s.body: The 1970s brought intense UK inflation pressure, with energy shocks, wage disputes, and policy changes weakening the purchasing power of pounds.

2020s.title: Recent UK price surge
2020s.body: The early 2020s saw rapid UK price increases, especially around energy, food, and household costs. The MVP reflects this through the ONS long-run RPI source.
